architecture_amendment_rule: A downstream change requires a reviewed amendment naming superseded rules and preserving audit history.

decisions.title: Layer and dependency direction
decisions.decision: Freeze domain, runtime, persistence, adapters, routes, projections, and web as separate layers. Domain remains pure; runtime orchestrates ports; persistence owns SQL and transactions; routes own auth/scope/validation; web remains read-only runtime presentation.
decisions.rationale: This prevents database, wall-clock, HTTP, and UI concerns from becoming physical-State semantics while retaining useful pure builders.
rejected_alternatives: Fastify routes compute or advance State
Domain builders read Postgres, environment, wall clock, filesystem, network, or random UUID
invariants: routes do not own generated-object writes
web cannot write State, Forecast, Scenario result, Checkpoint, or Activation

decisions.title: Shared runtime core and mode adapters
decisions.decision: Replay, Shadow-online, Controlled Field, and future Production share one semantic core. Only clock, ingress, scheduler, execution feedback, availability, and deployment vary through adapters.
decisions.rationale: Separate semantic implementations would make replay unable to validate online behavior and would recreate demo/runtime divergence.
rejected_alternatives: One semantic runtime per mode
Replay-only file persistence or mode-specific State semantics
invariants: same inputs and config yield the same semantic payload in every mode
Replay uses explicit time and no-future-leakage
Shadow-online has no AO-ACT creation authority

decisions.title: Canonical persistence and envelope profiles
decisions.decision: Postgres facts remain the only canonical append-only store. Every object declares record_class, history_class compatibility alias, lineage_member, envelope_profile, and transaction_families. LINEAGE requires lineage_id and revision_id; NON_LINEAGE_CONTEXT does not.
decisions.rationale: Append-only authority class and State-lineage membership answer different questions. Keeping them independent permits bootstrap and failure audit before a lineage exists without weakening deterministic lineage identity for State history.
rejected_alternatives: One shared envelope requiring lineage_id and revision_id for every append-only record
Infer lineage membership from record_class or history_class
invariants: indexes may upsert and must be rebuildable
record class does not imply lineage membership
non-lineage audit may exist before bootstrap, config, lineage, or checkpoint

decisions.title: State object granularity and atomicity
decisions.decision: A valid State advance atomically appends transition, assimilation update, and posterior State. The propagated prior is not current State; the posterior is canonical State.
decisions.rationale: Separate trace objects improve auditability while one transaction prevents partial prior/update/posterior histories.
rejected_alternatives: Independent canonical prior State
Latest sensor reading directly becomes root-zone State
invariants: prior is not canonical current State
sensor reading is Evidence, never direct State
transition, update, and posterior commit atomically

decisions.title: Tick claim, compute, and outcome transactions
decisions.decision: A tick has Claim, Compute, and Commit. A1 COMPLETED and A2 BLOCKED Forecast outcomes may commit State and checkpoint. Forecast FAILED is operational transaction F and creates no terminal tick or checkpoint.
decisions.rationale: A valid posterior can progress even when downstream Forecast prerequisites are unavailable, while numerical or invariant failure must not fabricate a completed tick or move recovery state.
rejected_alternatives: Persist FAILED Forecast inside State Tick Commit and advance checkpoint
Drop Forecast failure diagnostics because the State Tick transaction rolled back
invariants: A2 BLOCKED has a zero-point Forecast and COMPLETED_WITH_LIMITATIONS tick
FAILED creates no canonical tick or checkpoint

decisions.title: Checkpoint, lease, fencing, restart, and backfill
decisions.decision: Each scope has one fenced writer. Successful A1 or A2 ticks append checkpoint and CAS latest. Failed attempt or failed Forecast leaves checkpoint unchanged. Restart backfills sequentially.
decisions.rationale: Lease expiry alone cannot stop a resumed stale worker; fencing and CAS establish commit authority, and sequential backfill preserves the chain.
rejected_alternatives: Lease or advisory lock without fencing
Parallel same-scope advancement or out-of-order backfill

decisions.title: Idempotency and deterministic identity
decisions.decision: Every semantic object has object_id, idempotency_key, and determinism_hash. Lineage and revision participate only when lineage_member is true. fact_id and audit timestamps are excluded.
decisions.rationale: Separating fact identity from semantic identity avoids circular hashing and permits exactly-once-equivalent results under at-least-once scheduling.
rejected_alternatives: Random UUID or fact_id as semantic object identity
Overwrite on idempotency conflict
invariants: same semantic work produces the same object identity and hash
fact_id, created_at, persisted_at, worker, and lease owner are excluded from determinism_hash
idempotency conflict is a hard failure

decisions.title: Late Evidence and revision lifecycle
decisions.decision: Late Evidence creates a revision run and candidate lineage through E1, appends progress or terminal status through E2, and promotes only through E3 after complete chain validation. Promotion appends authority and atomically switches active pointers.
decisions.rationale: Declaration, progress, and promotion are distinct append-only transitions. Explicit variants make the complete candidate lifecycle auditable while preventing declaration or failed recomputation from changing active reads.
rejected_alternatives: A promotion-only transaction family with no declaration or progress path
Mutable in-place revision progress or active-lineage switching without promotion authority

decisions.title: Forecast result and failure separation
decisions.decision: twin_forecast_run_v1 permits COMPLETED with exactly 72 points or BLOCKED with zero points and reason_codes. FAILED is forbidden on the lineage Forecast object and uses non-lineage twin_forecast_failure_v1 in transaction F.
decisions.rationale: BLOCKED describes a valid posterior with an unavailable downstream Forecast prerequisite and may accompany State progress. FAILED describes failed computation and must remain operational audit so it cannot advance checkpoint or enter the active Forecast lineage.
rejected_alternatives: Treat BLOCKED and FAILED as the same canonical Forecast status
Persist FAILED twin_forecast_run_v1 inside State Tick Commit
invariants: COMPLETED advances checkpoint and successful Forecast index
BLOCKED advances checkpoint but not successful Forecast index
FAILED advances neither tick nor checkpoint
t0 is not a Forecast point

decisions.title: Scenario source eligibility
decisions.decision: Scenario is a separate atomic aggregate and may use only a committed COMPLETED Forecast with exactly 72 points and horizons 1 through 72. BLOCKED and FAILED outcomes are ineligible.
decisions.rationale: Comparable Scenario trajectories require a complete forecast horizon. Allowing blocked or failed results would create fabricated or non-comparable futures and blur Scenario with runtime failure handling.
rejected_alternatives: Generate Scenario from a zero-point BLOCKED Forecast
Generate Scenario directly from Forecast failure diagnostics
invariants: BLOCKED or FAILED Forecast cannot source Scenario

decisions.title: Residual and calibration separation
decisions.decision: Assimilation residual belongs to the current update. Forecast residual compares later Evidence with a historical COMPLETED Forecast point and never mutates the Forecast.
decisions.rationale: The two residuals have different temporal and governance roles; conflation would corrupt history and silently change models.
rejected_alternatives: One residual object for assimilation and forecast verification
Residual directly changes active model or historical forecast
invariants: forecast residual does not mutate Forecast

decisions.title: Model/config binding and activation
decisions.decision: Each tick resolves one immutable config before compute. Candidate, shadow evaluation, approval, and activation are separate append-only transitions. Activation changes only at a tick boundary.
decisions.rationale: A tick must not mix versions, and mutable active pointers need append-only authority and later consumption proof.
rejected_alternatives: Read active parameters repeatedly during a tick
Candidate self-activation or rollback by deleting history
invariants: activation changes only at tick boundary

decisions.title: Read API and human write API separation
decisions.decision: Runtime Operator APIs remain read-only under the field-scoped family. Generated objects have no public write endpoint. Human decision and action writes remain separately governed.
decisions.rationale: Extending the existing field-scoped family avoids a parallel namespace and prevents browser submission of generated objects.
rejected_alternatives: New /operator/twin-runtime/fields frontend family
Public HTTP tick or generated-object write endpoints
invariants: manual tick begins as application service, CLI/manual runner, and scheduler adapter

decisions.title: Legacy compatibility and migration
decisions.decision: Legacy paths remain read-only compatibility surfaces with no new MCFT capability or dual canonical write. Deletion requires caller migration and explicit approval.
decisions.rationale: Active callers and read contracts make immediate deletion unsafe, while continued feature growth would create dual mainlines.
rejected_alternatives: Delete legacy routes in DT-02
Dual-write legacy and new canonical State or Scenario
invariants: legacy paths gain no new capability

decisions.title: Runtime security and write authority
decisions.decision: Write authority is object-specific. Runtime Orchestrator writes State, Tick, and Forecast outcomes; Forecast failure uses operational audit; Revision Service owns E1 and E2; Promotion Service owns E3; Action Feedback Service may normalize trustworthy executed Evidence before formal Acceptance.
decisions.rationale: Object-specific authority and orthogonal execution/validation states prevent browser-generated runtime facts, action-stage collapse, and loss of timely executed Evidence.
rejected_alternatives: Require formal Acceptance before any executed Evidence can enter a later Evidence Window
Require every Action Feedback record to originate from AO-ACT
invariants: decision, approval, execution, and validation remain separate
Acceptance is not prerequisite for all trustworthy executed Evidence

decisions.title: Runtime health and claim boundaries
decisions.decision: Attempt, Health, and Forecast failure are non-lineage operational audit. They may exist before active lineage or checkpoint. Blocked or failed attempts do not become State history.
decisions.rationale: Failures, bootstrap problems, and recovery must be auditable without inventing lineage identity, failed State ticks, or agronomic meaning for runtime availability.
rejected_alternatives: Require active lineage and checkpoint before recording any Attempt or Health event
Represent Forecast failure as a canonical State-lineage Forecast
invariants: stale writer cannot report committed success
